channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Clawdbot/Moltbot/OpenClaw is cool, but it gets pricey fast
item.description: Rarely have I more appreciated the chasm between me and Silicon Valley than I have while using OpenClaw. This new AI program, which previously went by Moltbot and before that Clawdbot, has achieved virality over the past week for its ability to control your digital life via text message.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: After criticism, Microsoft promises big improvements for Windows 11
item.description: Windows developers are now focusing on improving the core experience in Windows 11, with Microsoft reportedly redirecting resources to address the operating system’s performance and reliability issues, according to The Verge.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Under Armour data breach claims trigger alerts for millions of users
item.description: Under Armour allegedly faces massive data breach affecting 72 million customers after hackers posted records online following November ransomware attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Should you eat snow cream? Opinions differ on the social-media-fueled winter confection
item.description: A major winter storm is expected to bring heavy snow to parts of the East Coast this weekend. Amid freezing temperatures, many will be hunkering down and sipping hot cocoa by the fire or trying out new warming winter recipes.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Waymo probed after robotaxi hit child near California elementary school
item.description: While police characterized the incident as a “non-injury collision,” Waymo told regulators that the child sustained minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: When government stops looking ahead, risks multiply: New survey shows foresight fading fast
item.description: "I think this is really an essential next step as we look to continuing the transformation that's going on in the government right now," Cunzeman said.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: In Rochester, pay phones are working again—and they’re free
item.description: Two years ago, the last pay phones were disconnected in Rochester, New York. But a group of volunteers has started bringing a handful of phones back—and making them free to use.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Commerce Department plans national AI center in San Francisco
item.description: The Trump administration is looking to increase the country’s edge in AI and defense, industries that are largely based in California.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: How to reduce Windows driver bloat
item.description: Over time on Windows systems, it’s inevitable that old device drivers get supplanted by new ones. Even on systems where drivers aren’t rigorously maintained, Windows Update will typically update at least a dozen drivers annually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Big Tech CEOs’ tortured statements on ICE in Minnesota show an industry captured by Trump
item.description: On the morning of January 24 in downtown Minneapolis, an ICE agent shot and killed protester Alex Pretti, an ICU nurse at a local Veterans Affairs hospital. Just 2 miles away, on January 7, another ICE agent had shot and killed Nicole Renee Good, a mother.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google adds AI image generation to Chrome browser, side panel option for virtual assistant
item.description: Google is empowering its Chrome browser with the ability to alter pictures and introducing a side panel for a virtual assistant to help with online tasks as part of its push to turbocharge its of digital services with more artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5 myths about identity theft that put your data at risk
item.description: Identity theft myths debunked: your personal data is already exposed on data broker sites, and scammers don't need major breaches to target you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spanish soccer league offers reward of 50 euros for tips to ID bars illegally broadcasting games
item.description: The Spanish league says it is stepping up its fight against audio-visual fraud by offering 50 euros for each verified tip on the establishments broadcasting games without proper permission.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gemini Flash model gets visual reasoning capability
item.description: Google has added an Agentic Vision capability to its Gemini 3 Flash model, which the company said combines visual reasoning with code execution to ground answers in visual evidence. The capability fundamentally changes how AI models process images, according to Google.
item.dc:creator: Info World
